steps.title: Add water
steps.instruction: Add 400 mL of MilliQ water to a 1000 mL beaker.

steps.title: Set up stirring and heating
steps.instruction: Add stir bar in beaker and set on hot plate at 50 °C.

steps.title: Add NaOH
steps.instruction: Add 5 drops (500ul) of 10N NaOH (this makes the solution basic to help dissolve the paraformaldehyde).

steps.title: Add paraformaldehyde and stir
steps.instruction: Weigh 20 g of Paraformaldehyde and add to MilliQ water. Stir for about 1 hour or until all paraformaldehyde is dissolved and solution is clear.

steps.title: Prepare 1M Phosphate buffer (pH 7.4)
steps.instruction: Make 1 Molarity (M) Phosphate buffer at PH 7.4. (Details for buffer preparation are in subsequent steps).

steps.title: Adjust volume
steps.instruction: Top off to 500ml with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Filter solution
steps.instruction: Filter solution through .2um CA member filter.

steps.title: Label and store
steps.instruction: Label container with '4% paraformaldehyde in .1M PB' and date. Store at 4 °C for up to 1 week. Can also be aliquoted into Falcon Tube (50 mL) and stored at -20 °C for 3 months.

steps.title: Prepare Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate stock
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 Molarity (M) Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate. Store stock at Room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare Sodium Phosphate Monobasic Anhydrous stock
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 Molarity (M) Sodium Phosphate Monobasic Anhydrous. Store stock at Room temperature.

steps.title: Combine for 1M PB stock
steps.instruction: To make 100 mL, 1M PB at PH 7.4 add 19 mL Monobasic plus 81 mL Dibasic. Store as stock at Room temperature.

steps.title: Add PB to PFA solution
steps.instruction: Add 50 mL, 1M PB PH 7.4 to paraformaldehyde solution.

steps.title: Adjust pH
steps.instruction: Test PH and adjust solution to final desired pH using HCl or NaOH.
